3.why: We need the dimensions to scan the grid.

4.why: We need the width to scan each row.

5.why: We must read the original values after edits begin.
5.invariant: The snapshot will mirror the input.

6.happening: Take a source row to copy.
6.invariant: Snapshot rows added one at a time.

7.happening: Store a copy of that row.
7.why: Editing matrix later must not change what we read.
7.invariant: The snapshot stays frozen at the input.

8.why: Look for zeros in the frozen snapshot.

9.why: Check each cell of this row.

10.happening: Was the snapshot cell at ({row},{col}) a zero?
10.invariant: We read the snapshot, never the live matrix.

11.why: A zero blanks its entire row.

13.why: A zero blanks its entire column.

15.why: Every original zero has cleared its row and column.
15.invariant: matrix is fully zeroed where required.

init.happening: Read dimensions and start the snapshot.
init.why: Prepare to scan a frozen copy.

loop.why: Visit each cell or sweep a row/column.

check.happening: Test a snapshot cell for zero.

update.happening: Copy a row or blank a cell.
update.why: Build the snapshot, then clear rows and columns.
